turns.human: How did the west do last quarter?
replies:
I need to clarify a couple of things before I can form a good query. "The west" and "last quarter" are underspecified in this manifold, and "how did it do" could mean several measures.

A couple of questions:

1. **"The west"** — the manifold has a `region` level, but I don't know its members. Do you mean a specific region value (e.g. one named "West")? If so, what's the exact region name?

2. **"How did it do"** — which measure(s) are you after? Options include `revenue`, `orders`, `buyers`, `units_sold`, `units_returned`, or `stock`.

3. **"Last quarter"** — do you have a specific quarter in mind (e.g. a `cal.quarter` value), or a relative "most recent" one?

Let me know and I'll propose the right query.

turns.human: How are we doing on returns?

replies:
The results above show your return rate (units returned ÷ units sold) by month for this year.

Want to slice it differently — say by product, category, region, or store — or look at raw returned units instead of a rate? Just let me know.

history.text: How are we doing on returns?
"How are we doing on returns?" is a bit open-ended. Let me check what's available so I can propose something sensible.

Your question is open-ended, so let me offer a natural default. "How are we doing on returns" usually means a return rate over time. There's no prebuilt `return_rate` measure, but I can build it from `units_returned / units_sold`. Both live in the transaction universe, so the expression is legal.

Before I run it, a quick question on grain: do you want an overall figure, or a trend? I'll propose a monthly trend as the most useful default — let me verify it first.
[call explain {"frameql": "SELECT units_returned / units_sold AS return_rate AT {cal.month}"}]
This will serve cleanly. I'll show returns as a monthly trend (units returned ÷ units sold).
[call query {"frameql": "SELECT units_returned / units_sold AS return_rate AT {cal.month}"}]
engine: outcome=serve; served columns [return_rate]. (values shown to the human)

The results above show your return rate (units returned ÷ units sold) by month for this year.

Want to slice it differently — say by product, category, region, or store — or look at raw returned units instead of a rate? Just let me know.
